title: Give terminal detail a dedicated URL, restored across refresh and login
action: Give the terminal-detail route a dedicated, refreshable URL per docs/history/pbi-025-terminal-detail-url/CONTEXT.md D1-D5. Construct the URL as a path segment /terminal/<pane_id> (D1) whenever navigate() renders {name:'terminal'}; every other route (login, switcher) resolves to '/' (D4, no distinct URL for them). Use history.pushState when moving forward into a new route and history.back() (never a fresh pushState) for in-app Back, with a popstate listener that re-renders whichever route the resulting URL parses to -- so browser Back / phone swipe-back match in-app Back exactly, never requiring two presses (D2). In bootstrap(), parse location.pathname: on a /terminal/<pane_id> match, call fetchAgents() and look up pane_id in both agents[] and shells[]; an AgentRow hit opens {name:'terminal', agent} directly, a ShellRow hit is turned into a NewPaneRef the same way switcher.ts's onSelect does ({pane_id, workspace_id, label: path ?? workspace_label}) before opening terminal detail; no match (closed pane, stale/bad link) falls back silently to switcher, no toast or error text (D3). Preserve the parsed target across a login round-trip (D5): when bootstrap's initial fetchAgents() probe fails, keep the parsed intended route in a local variable and have renderLogin's onSuccess closure re-resolve it against a fresh fetchAgents() call and navigate there, falling back to switcher if the pane no longer resolves -- do not change LoginProps's onSuccess signature (still () => void).

truths: Opening /terminal/<pane_id> directly (valid session, pane present in agents[] or shells[]) renders terminal detail for that pane without visiting switcher first.
A stale or nonexistent pane_id in the URL falls back to switcher with no error UI (D3).
Selecting an agent/shell from switcher, or a successful create-flow, updates the URL to /terminal/<pane_id> via pushState (D1/D2).
In-app Back and the browser/phone Back button both return from terminal detail to switcher and to switcher's URL ('/'), in exactly one step (D2).
A login prompted by opening a stale-session terminal URL redirects into that same terminal on success, when the pane still resolves (D5).

artifacts.substantive: adds URL construction/parsing, pushState/popstate wiring, pane_id-to-route resolution, and login-redirect preservation -- no stub/TODO logic

new test file covering D1-D5: URL construction/parsing round-trip, pushState vs back() semantics, pane resolution for both AgentRow and ShellRow, stale pane_id fallback, and login-redirect preservation

key_links: main.ts's navigate() calls history.pushState/history.back() consistently with the popstate listener
bootstrap()'s login branch wires onSuccess to the preserved intended route, not a hardcoded navigate({name:'switcher'})
prohibitions: No server-side route changes (SPA fallback in src/web/mod.rs already returns index.html for any unmatched path)
No change to LoginProps or TerminalProps public signatures
No dedicated URL added for switcher or login (D4; that is PBI-033, out of scope)

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Terminal detail now has a dedicated, refreshable /terminal/<pane_id> URL: pushState on forward nav, history.back() for in-app Back with a popstate listener keeping browser/phone Back in sync (D1/D2); bootstrap parses the URL and resolves pane_id against agents[]/shells[], falling back silently to switcher on no match (D3); switcher/login stay at '/' (D4); a login triggered by a stale-session terminal URL redirects back into that terminal on success via a closure variable, LoginProps signature unchanged (D5).
